  "decision": "fail",

  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],

  "value": 0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Click wrench used upside down, which can alter the torque mechanism's accuracy.",
    "Wrench is uncalibrated, violating OEM/engineering specs for torque accuracy."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately.",
    "Replace with a calibrated torque wrench used in the correct orientation.",
    "Verify calibration status and orientation before resuming fastening.",
    "Re-torque any fasteners already tightened with the defective tool."
  ],
  "rationale": "Using an uncalibrated click wrench upside down introduces unknown torque errors, risking under- or over-tightening. This is non-compliant with OEM specs and can lead to joint failure, making the work unsafe and unacceptable.",
  "references": ["OEM/engineering specs for torque tool calibration and usage", "ASME B107.300 (Torque Instruments)", "ISO 6789 (Torque tool calibration)"]
}
